LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LITE-ON TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY CASE ON GROUND OF INCONVENIENT FORUM [C.C.P. 418.10(A)(2), 410.30(A)]. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 21, 2010 ON THE COURT'S MOTION. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LITE-ON TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY CASE ON GROUND OF INCONVENIENT FORUM [CCP 418.10(A)(2), 410.30(A)] IS GRANTED. THIS CASE IS STAYED PENDING DISPOSITION OF THE TAIWANESE ACTION. ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE: WHETHER BY THE PENDING MOTION TO DISMISS IN THE TAIWANESE ACTION OR OTHERWISE, WITH THE COURT RETAINING JURISDICTION OF THIS ACTION PENDING FURTHER MOTION OF THE PARTIES OR ORDER OF THE COURT. CALIFORNIA HAS LITTLE INTEREST IN THIS CASE WHICH INVOLVES FOREIGN COMPANIES. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THERE ARE NUMEROUS WITNESSES AND ALMOST ALL OF THEM ARE OUT OF STATE. TAIWAN IS A SUITABLE ALTERNATIVE FORUM. SEE STANGVIK V. SHILEY, INC. (1991) 54 CAL 3RD. 744. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-21-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Case On Ground Of Inconvenient Forum [Ccp 418.10(A)(2), 410.30(A)]
Off Calendar Dec-21-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph A. Greco, Esq., and Alfredo A. Bismonte, Esq.,Beck, Ross, Bismonte & Finley, LLP, 408-938-7900, for defendant and moving party: Lite-On Technology Corporation
Chris Scott Graham, Esq., Dechert, LLP, 650-813-4860, for plaintiff and opposing party: Inpro II Licensing Sarl, a Luxembourg Limited Liability Company

Argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as modified. DEFENDANT LITE-ON TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Case On Ground Of Inconvenient Forum [Ccp 418.10(A)(2), 410.30(A)]. GRANTED. This case is stayed pending disposition of the Taiwanese action.
Additional language: Whether by the Pending motion to dismiss in the Taiwanese action or otherwise, with the court retaining jurisdiction of this action pending further motion of the parties or order of the court.
California has little interest in this case which involves foreing companies. The evidence shows there are numerous witnesses and almost all of them are out of State. Taiwan is a suitable alternative forum. See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal 3rd. 744. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.). (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Case On Ground Of Inconvenient Forum [Ccp 418.10(A)(2), 410.30(A)]
